ocr.text: In the study by Shah et al. [106] conducted in 1991 involving 7 PD patients, the antioxidant used was 100 mg ascorbic acid/day
over a study period of 4 weeks, resulting in ↑ 45% in serum ascorbic acid levels. Ando et al. [100] in 1999 studied 16 CAPD
patients with 1.8 gr EPA/day for 12 weeks, which led to ↓ serum ox-LDL levels. Islam et al. [101] in 2000 examined 17 PD
patients, administering 800 IU AT/day for 12 weeks, resulting in ↓ susceptibility of LDL oxidation, ↑ 217% serum lipid-
standardized AT, and ↑ 135% serum LDL AT levels. O' Byrne et al. [104] in 2001 also studied 17 PD patients with 800 IU
AT/day for 12 weeks, achieving ↑ serum LDL AT levels with no effect on autoantibodies to ox-LDL. Diepeveen et al. [102] in
2005 researched 21 PD patients receiving 40 mg atorvastatin/day over 12 weeks, resulting in ↓ serum ox-LDL levels, improved
lipid profile, and no effect on in vitro LDL oxidizability. Another study in 2005 with 800 IU AT/day over 12 weeks noted no
effect on serum ox-LDL levels, no effect on lipid profile, and ↓ in vitro LDL oxidizability. Domenici et al.
Study  Year  Patient modality  Antioxidant  Study period  Results
Shah et al. [106]  1991  7 PD  100 mg ascorbic acid/day  4 weeks  ↑ 45% in serum ascorbic acid levels
Ando et al. [100]  1999  16 CAPD  1.8 gr EPA/day  12 weeks  ↓ serum ox-LDL levels
Islam et al. [101]  2000  17 PD  800 IU AT/day  12 weeks  ↓ susceptibility of LDL oxidation↑ 217% serum lipid-standardized AT↑ 135% serum LDL AT levels
O' Byrne et al. [104]  2001  17 PD  800 IU AT/day  12 weeks  ↑ serum LDL AT levelsNo effect on autoantibodies to ox-LDL
Diepeveen et al. [102]  2005  21 PD  40 mg atorvastatin/day  12 weeks  ↓ serum ox-LDL levelsImproved lipid profileNo effect on in vitro LDL oxidizability
800 IU AT/day  12 weeks  No effect on serum ox-LDL levelsNo effect on lipid profile↓ in vitro LDL oxidizability
Domenici et al. [103]  2005  22 PD  300 mg AT x3/week  4 weeks  ↑ 50% serum vitamin E levels↓ protein oxidation↓ OS-induced DNA damage (serum levels 8-OHdG)
Uzum et al. [23]  2006  13 PD  300 mg AT/day  20 weeks  ↑ serum vitamin E levels↓ erythrocyte osmotic fragility
Nascimento et al. [117]  2010  30 PD  1200 mg NAC/day  8 weeks  ↑ serum NAC levels (2.6 to 24.8 mmol/l)↓ serum IL-6 levels(9.4 to 7.6 pg/ml)
Boudouris et al. [15]  2013  20 CAPD  250 mg vitamin C/day400 IU AT/day  8 weeks  ↑ serum, urine, and peritoneal fluid TAC levels↓ serum, urine, and peritoneal fluid MDA levels
[103] in 2005 with 22 PD patients taking 300 mg AT x3/week for 4 weeks saw ↑ 50% serum vitamin E levels, ↓ protein
oxidation, and ↓ OS-induced DNA damage (serum levels 8-OHdG). Uzum et al. [23] in 2006 studied 13 PD patients with 300
mg AT/day for 20 weeks, resulting in ↑ serum vitamin E levels and ↓ erythrocyte osmotic fragility. Nascimento et al. [117] in
2010 involved 30 PD patients with 1200 mg NAC/day for 8 weeks, leading to ↑ serum NAC levels (2.6 to 24.8 mmol/l) and ↓
serum IL-6 levels (9.4 to 7.6 pg/ml). Lastly, Boudouris et al. [15] in 2013 examined 20 CAPD patients with 250 mg vitamin
C/day and 400 IU AT/day for 8 weeks, resulting in ↑ serum, urine, and peritoneal fluid TAC levels and ↓ serum, urine, and
peritoneal fluid MDA levels.
